commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer/product review plus contributor live proof, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair lane.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, token logging, or endpoint-validation regression was found; OAuth credential writes remain an auth-provider merge risk and proof gate rather than a concrete security defect. (sha=3b79341e18008f7b9191dc8fd1acc2e3ec71c988)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Kimi Code OAuth credential reuse and refresh, Kimi/Pi local token-cost scanning, Kimi tier/source labels, settings toggles, CLI cost support, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the Kimi OAuth and local Kimi cost-reporting paths, and AGENTS.md discourages unrequested live provider probes.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer/product review plus contributor live proof, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: Improve Kimi Code usage reporting
target.body:
## Summary

Improves Kimi Code usage reporting while keeping Kimi Code separate from Moonshot, Kimi K2, and generic Kimi API providers.

- Reuses `~/.kimi-code/credentials/kimi-code.json` in auto mode and reports the active source.
- Preserves Kimi quota windows and shows Session before Weekly.
- Adds local Kimi Code `wire.jsonl` and Pi Kimi session usage behind settings toggles.
- Keeps explicit API mode API-key only.
- Keeps generic Pi provider `kimi` out of Kimi Code accounting; only Kimi Code-specific provider IDs qualify.
- Preserves existing Pi cost-history behavior when callers omit source options.
- Invalidates stale Pi cost caches after provider-attribution changes.

## Maintainer audit

Maintainer fixes `84640a376` and `3b79341e1` close four correctness/security gaps found during review:

1. CLI-owned Kimi OAuth bearer and refresh tokens are no longer forwarded when `KIMI_CODE_BASE_URL`, `KIMI_CODE_OAUTH_HOST`, or `KIMI_OAUTH_HOST` overrides are present. Endpoint overrides require an explicit API key.
2. Generic Pi provider ID `kimi` is no longer attributed to Kimi Code.
3. `CostUsageSourceOptions()` keeps the existing Pi-session default enabled; the new Kimi source remains disabled by default at the core API boundary. The Pi cache schema is bumped so old attribution is not reused.
4. Expired OAuth refreshes now send the Kimi-compatible `User-Agent` and `X-Msh-*` device identity headers and reuse/persist `KIMI_CODE_HOME/device_id`.

Unknown CLI credential fields and restrictive file permissions remain preserved by the proposed refresh writer.

## Upstream contract evidence

The implementation follows current official Kimi Code contracts at `62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94`:

- [credential storage, permissions, and multi-process locking](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-code/blob/62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94/packages/oauth/src/storage.ts)
- [OAuth refresh behavior](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-code/blob/62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94/packages/oauth/src/oauth.ts)
- [device identity headers and stable device ID](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-code/blob/62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94/packages/oauth/src/identity.ts)
- [managed usage endpoint](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-code/blob/62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94/packages/oauth/src/managed-usage.ts)
- [`usage.record` write and scope contract](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-code/blob/62999caca3b56d865bb44f1f0336bff942765d94/packages/agent-core/src/agent/usage/index.ts)

Current upstream records normal model calls as `turn` and real compaction model calls as `session`; both are additive usage. The scanner therefore correctly includes both scopes rather than dropping compaction cost.

## Privacy and provider isolation

Local scanning aggregates token-usage records; CodexBar does not display raw prompts. Quota and plan data remain Kimi-sourced. Local model-cost values are estimates, not authoritative subscription billing. The OAuth path is disabled for custom API/OAuth hosts, preventing CLI-owned credentials from crossing origins.

## Validation

Exact maintainer head: `3b79341e1`

- focused Kimi/cost suites: 73 tests passed on the baseline maintainer fix;
- exact-head `swift test --filter KimiSettingsReaderTests`: 20 tests passed;
- exact-head `make check`: passed (SwiftFormat + strict SwiftLint);
- exact-head `make test`: passed, 43/43 shards;
- exact-head whole-branch and uncommitted structured autoreviews: no actionable findings;
- exact-head `CODEXBAR_SIGNING=adhoc ./Scripts/package_app.sh`: passed; packaged `CodexBar.app`;
- all review threads resolved;
- `git diff --check`: passed;
- exact-head GitHub CI: running.

No real Kimi Code credential, Kimi local sessions, or Pi Kimi session records are available in the authorized test environment. Therefore the API/OAuth response shape, refresh-file interoperability under concurrent CLI use, and rendered real-account values do not yet have exact live proof.

## Landing decision

**Recommendation: hold; do not merge yet.** Two product/ownership decisions and one proof gate remain:

1. **CLI-owned OAuth writes.** Upstream Kimi uses scoped credential slots, retry handling, and a multi-process lock. This branch refreshes and rewrites the CLI-owned credential file without equivalent cross-process coordination. Safest landing shape: read valid CLI credentials only; require an explicit CodexBar/API key after expiry. Alternatively, implement upstream-equivalent lock/retry/scoping and prove concurrent CLI + CodexBar refresh live.
2. **Local scan default.** The app preference currently enables new Kimi Code session scanning for users who have never chosen the setting. Recommend opt-in (`false`) unless the product decision is explicitly “automatic local analysis.”
3. **Live proof.** Exercise API-key usage, fresh OAuth reuse, expired OAuth refresh, real Kimi Code `wire.jsonl`, and real Pi Kimi records; verify account identity/plan isolation and refresh latency with redacted output.

This branch also overlaps open cost-scanner PR #1797. Do not rebase or resolve that overlap until #1797 lands or is closed; then rerun exact-head review/tests/CI.
target.author: leechael

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
